seoTitle: Hexham vs High Range — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Hexham and High Range. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Hexham vs High Range: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hexham and High Range.
heading: Hexham vs High Range — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hexham, New South Wales and High Range, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: High Range has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Hexham (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hexham or High Range safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hexham has a flood risk score of 36/100, while High Range scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hexham scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. High Range scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hexham or High Range?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. High Range has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.93% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
